goal:
Create a complete, fully playable two-player chess game with a beautiful, polished interface, split into exactly 3 files: index.html, style.css, and script.js. No external chess libraries — implement all chess logic from scratch in vanilla JS.
Core Chess Logic (must be fully correct)

Standard 8x8 board, all pieces, correct starting position.
Legal move generation for every piece type: pawn (including double-step from start, diagonal captures, en passant), knight, bishop, rook, queen, king.
Castling (kingside and queenside) with all legality checks (king/rook haven't moved, squares between are empty, king not passing through/landing on attacked squares).
Pawn promotion with a UI popup to choose Queen/Rook/Bishop/Knight.
Check, checkmate, and stalemate detection.
Move validation that prevents a player from making a move that leaves their own king in check.
Turn alternation between White and Black (2 human players sharing the same screen/device, no AI).
Draw conditions: stalemate, threefold repetition (optional bonus), insufficient material (optional bonus).
Move history log displayed in standard algebraic notation (e.g. e4, Nf3, O-O, Qxd5+, e8=Q).

Interface & UX ("lovely")

Elegant centered board with a wooden or marble-style gradient for light/dark squares (no images required — use CSS gradients/colors), subtle border and shadow around the board, rank/file labels (1–8, a–h) along the edges.
Pieces rendered as crisp Unicode chess glyphs (♔♕♖♗♘♙) sized generously, with smooth CSS transitions when moved (pieces glide to their destination square rather than snapping).
Clicking a piece highlights it and shows all legal destination squares as soft glowing dots/circles; clicking an occupied enemy square that's a legal capture shows a subtle highlighted ring instead.
Last-moved squares stay subtly highlighted until the next move.
King in check pulses with a soft red glow on its square.
Captured pieces displayed in two small "tray" areas on the side, grouped by type, with a tiny fade-in animation as each is captured.
Turn indicator showing whose move it is, with a small animated icon/swatch matching the active color.
Move history panel on the side, scrollable, auto-scrolling to the latest move, styled like a neat scorecard.
Checkmate/stalemate end screen as an elegant modal overlay with a "New Game" button, fade/scale-in animation.
A "New Game" / "Resign" / "Offer Draw" button row, nicely styled with hover effects.
Subtle sound-free animation cues: gentle square-flash on capture, soft shake animation on illegal move attempt.
Fully responsive layout — board scales gracefully on smaller screens, side panels collapse below the board on mobile.

Code Organization

index.html: semantic markup only — board container, side panels (history, captured pieces, controls), modal overlay markup. Links to style.css and script.js.
style.css: all visual design — board grid/squares, piece transitions, highlights, panels, modal, responsive breakpoints, color palette (recommend a refined palette like deep walnut + cream, or slate + ivory).
script.js: organized into clear sections — board state representation (8x8 array), move generation per piece type, legality/check-detection helpers, game state machine (turn, history, captured pieces, game-over conditions), rendering/DOM sync functions, event handlers (click-to-select, click-to-move), and the new-game/reset logic.

Quality Bar

No bugs in legal move generation — this is the most important requirement; test edge cases like en passant, castling through check, pinned pieces, and discovered checks mentally before finalizing logic.
Clean, commented code with descriptive function names.
Smooth 60fps-feeling animations using CSS transitions, not janky JS-driven repositioning.

subtasks.title: UI Layout and Styling
subtasks.summary: Implemented the UI layout and styling. Created index.html with the board, sidebar, and promotion modal. Added responsive CSS in styles.css for different screen sizes and game states. Initialized the basic game structure in script.js, including board rendering and basic interaction logic.

subtasks.title: Board State and Piece Initialization
subtasks.summary: Implemented Board class with initial piece placement, 2D array representation, and DOM rendering logic. Integrated with HTML and CSS for a basic visual board.

subtasks.title: Basic Move Validation
subtasks.summary: Implemented basic move validation for all chess pieces (Pawn, Knight, Bishop, Rook, Queen, King) in `src/moves.js`, including path obstruction checks for sliding pieces. Integrated these validators into the `Game` class to enable basic piece movement on the board.
